However, because we are modeling our uncertainty about the population-level parameter (and not the variation across individual companies), we allow for moderate uncertainty.

To translate these beliefs into Beta parameters we want:

• Some moderate effective sample size (α+β) that reflects neither complete ignorance (which would lead to a uniform Beta(1,1)) nor over–confidence. Choosing α+β = 10 provides a moderate amount of precision.

The resulting variance is (αβ)/[(α+β)²(α+β+1)] = (7×3)/(100×11) ≈ 0.019, leading to a moderate spread (with an approximate 95% credible interval roughly from 0.42 to 0.97).
